purpose: Integrate browser-use as a specialized research/web-automation worker path under hub control with strict policy, auditable execution, bounded recovery loops, and artifact-based verification.
goal: Enable reliable browser automation for research and web tasks without breaking the hub-worker architecture, security controls, or deterministic fallback policy.
program_success_criteria: Hub remains the central owner of routing, policy decisions, and task orchestration.
Browser-use is introduced as a specialized worker/backend path, never as worker-to-worker orchestration.
Every browser task has explicit domain, action-budget, timeout, and download policy constraints.
Security violations are hard-blocked, while recoverable tool/schema issues are handled as recoverable states.
Research/browser outputs are stored as verifiable artifacts with provenance and evidence.
End-to-end tests prove browser task completion and policy enforcement.
Operational telemetry shows if browser path, LLM path, and fallback path were used.

tasks.title: Add browser task contract extension
acceptance_criteria: Task/goal contract supports browser-specific fields: allowed_domains, max_actions, timeout_seconds, download_policy, auth_policy, screenshot_policy.
Contract is backward compatible for existing non-browser tasks.
Contract version and source are persisted in metadata.
Unit tests validate defaults and schema validation failures.

tasks.title: Add hub routing decision for browser path
acceptance_criteria: Hub routing can choose browser backend for research/web_automation tasks.
Routing decision records reason and selected backend.
No worker initiates downstream orchestration on its own.
Tests cover route-to-browser and route-to-default decisions.

tasks.title: Define task kind and intent mapping
acceptance_criteria: Canonical mapping exists from intents to browser actions (navigate, click, type, extract, screenshot).
Mapping does not override coding/file tool intents.
Ambiguous mappings produce recoverable needs_review status.
Mapping is documented in code and track docs.

tasks.title: Add config surface for browser backend
acceptance_criteria: Config supports provider mode (oss/cloud), command/SDK settings, timeouts, and enable flags.
Config read model shows configured/enabled/preflight state.
Invalid configs are surfaced as explicit preflight errors.
Default config remains disabled-safe.

tasks.title: Implement domain allowlist enforcement
acceptance_criteria: Browser execution blocks navigation outside allowed_domains.
Wildcard/domain rules are normalized and validated.
Violations are hard-blocked with policy audit event.
Tests cover allow, deny, and malformed allowlist entries.

tasks.title: Implement action budget and timeout limits
acceptance_criteria: Per-task max_actions and timeout are enforced by hub policy and execution layer.
Exceeded budgets stop execution deterministically.
Stop reason is persisted and visible in status/audit.
Tests cover budget exhaustion and timeout expiration.

tasks.title: Implement download and file-write policy
acceptance_criteria: Download policy supports deny, whitelist, and bounded output directory mode.
Downloaded files outside allowed scope are blocked.
All write events include provenance and policy decision in audit.
Tests cover denied and allowed download flows.

tasks.title: Implement credential and auth guardrails
acceptance_criteria: No plain secret injection into prompts or logs by default.
Auth usage requires explicit contract opt-in and masked audit metadata.
Sensitive fields are redacted in status and trace output.
Tests cover redaction and blocked unauthorized auth usage.

tasks.title: Implement browser-use execution adapter
acceptance_criteria: Adapter supports session creation, action execution, and result collection.
Adapter can run in configured oss or cloud mode.
Adapter returns normalized execution result contract.
Failures are categorized as security, transient, or fatal.

tasks.title: Add adapter preflight and health checks
acceptance_criteria: Preflight verifies binary/sdk availability and required env config.
Health endpoint/report exposes readiness state.
Unready backend cannot be selected silently.
Tests cover ready and unready conditions.

tasks.title: Normalize browser action traces
acceptance_criteria: Each action records action_type, target, outcome, duration, and policy_decision_ref.
Trace is correlated with goal_id, task_id, and execution trace_id.
Trace format is stable for downstream verification.
Tests validate trace schema and correlation fields.

tasks.title: Integrate adapter into task scoped execution service
acceptance_criteria: Research/web tasks can execute through browser adapter path.
Non-browser tasks keep existing execution behavior.
Backend_used and routing metadata are persisted.
Regression tests prove no break for existing research artifact flow.

tasks.title: Define browser artifact schema
acceptance_criteria: Artifact schema supports extracted_data, page_evidence, screenshots, sources, and citations.
Schema includes provenance fields per source.
Schema is validated before persistence.
Tests cover valid and invalid artifact payloads.

tasks.title: Persist browser artifacts with provenance
acceptance_criteria: Artifacts are persisted via tracking service with stable references.
Artifact references are linked to task history and trace.
Storage policy is compatible with existing artifact persistence model.
Tests cover persistence and retrieval of browser artifacts.

tasks.title: Add browser artifact verification gates
acceptance_criteria: Verification checks presence/quality of extracted output and source evidence.
Verification supports task-defined minimum source count or required evidence fields.
Failed verification blocks completion and emits actionable reason.
Tests cover pass and fail verification outcomes.

tasks.title: Add completion policy integration for browser tasks
acceptance_criteria: Browser tasks complete only after verification gates pass.
No completion on empty markdown-only output if evidence is required.
Completion payload includes artifact refs and verification summary.
Regression tests cover completion decisions.

tasks.title: Add bounded repair loop for browser failures
acceptance_criteria: Retry/repair loop is hub-controlled with max_repair_attempts.
Transient failures can be retried with adjusted non-security parameters.
Security denials do not auto-retry unless policy allows safe re-plan.
Tests cover successful repair and bounded terminal failure.

tasks.title: Classify browser failure taxonomy
acceptance_criteria: Failure classes include security_denied, policy_denied, transient_navigation, element_not_found, timeout, backend_unavailable.
Each class maps to block, retry, needs_review, or fail policy.
Classification appears in status and audit.
Tests cover taxonomy mapping.

tasks.title: Add deterministic fallback policy for browser path
acceptance_criteria: Fallback to non-browser deterministic flow is explicit and policy-driven.
Fallback usage is audited with reason and source policy version.
Fallback is disabled for tasks that strictly require browser evidence.
Tests cover fallback allowed and denied cases.

tasks.title: Add needs_review escalation contract
acceptance_criteria: Ambiguous or blocked browser outcomes can escalate with structured needs_review payload.
Payload includes policy reason, failure class, and evidence refs.
Escalation path avoids silent failed status for recoverable issues.
Tests cover escalation payload contract.

tasks.title: Add audit event family for browser execution
acceptance_criteria: Audit events cover route_selected, policy_checked, action_executed, policy_blocked, artifact_verified, fallback_used.
Events include original_intent, resolved_backend, and reason where relevant.
Event chain is reconstructable by trace_id.
Tests/smoke checks prove event coverage in one full run.

tasks.title: Add observability for browser backend usage
acceptance_criteria: Status/diagnostics show browser backend calls, action counts, last error class, and latency.
Output distinguishes browser path from default research path.
Metrics are safe and redacted.
Tests cover diagnostic payload format.

tasks.title: Add end-to-end tests for browser research tasks
acceptance_criteria: At least two E2E scenarios exist: allowed-domain extraction and blocked-domain attempt.
E2E validates artifact persistence and verification gates.
E2E validates policy hard-block behavior.
E2E is runnable in CI with deterministic fixtures or controlled mocks.

tasks.title: Document rollout, ops runbook, and migration steps
acceptance_criteria: Runbook documents enablement, required config, preflight troubleshooting, and policy tuning.
Migration notes explain backward compatibility and phased rollout.
Rollback procedure is documented and tested in dry run.
Docs link to architecture constraints from AGENTS.md.

evidence: New browser contract/policy/adapter/artifact/recovery services added under agent/services/browser_*.
Unit tests for new browser services added under tests/test_browser_*.py.
Generic routing/fallback/artifact/verification platform capabilities remain reusable but browser-path integration into task-scoped execution is still open.

status_interpretation.done: browser-use specific acceptance criteria implemented and test-covered
status_interpretation.in_progress: partial via existing generic platform capability, browser specialization still missing
status_interpretation.todo: no meaningful implementation detected for browser-use-specific scope

execution_stage_summary.stage_1: Define contract and routing so browser path is explicit and hub-owned.
execution_stage_summary.stage_2: Enforce security guardrails before any browser action.
execution_stage_summary.stage_3: Integrate adapter with normalized traces and backend preflight.
execution_stage_summary.stage_4: Persist verifiable artifacts and enforce completion gates.
execution_stage_summary.stage_5: Add bounded recovery and explicit fallback semantics.
execution_stage_summary.stage_6: Prove behavior with auditability, telemetry, and E2E coverage.

summary_notes: This track introduces browser-use as a specialized backend path, not a replacement for existing coding/research execution.
The highest risk is policy bypass through browser automation; domain and download controls are therefore P0 and critical.
Completion must remain evidence-based to avoid false positives.
Fallback behavior must be explicit and auditable.
All changes preserve hub-owned orchestration and worker-only execution responsibilities.

end_summaries.title: Architecture Summary
items: Hub decides if browser path is selected based on task kind and policy.
Worker executes browser actions only within contract limits.
Artifacts and verification remain first-class completion controls.

end_summaries.title: Security Summary
items: Allowed domains, action budgets, timeout limits, and download policy are mandatory guardrails.
Security violations are hard-blocked with auditable policy reasons.
Secrets and auth metadata are redacted by default.

end_summaries.title: Operations Summary
items: Preflight and diagnostics show browser backend readiness and usage.
Audit trail reconstructs route, actions, policy checks, and fallback decisions.
E2E scenarios prove both success and blocked behavior.

summaries.quick_recommendation: Start with BU-T001, BU-T002, BU-T005, BU-T009, BU-T013, BU-T021, BU-T023 in that order.
summaries.default_rollout_mode: Feature-flagged rollout with browser backend disabled by default and enabled per environment after preflight pass.
summaries.definition_of_done: Browser tasks run through hub policy, produce verified artifacts, and are fully auditable in E2E scenarios.
